transcript:
Agent: Thank you for calling StreamVault. My name is Nina. How can I assist you today?

Customer: I want to cancel my subscription. I have been a customer for four years but the price keeps going up and I do not use it enough to justify the cost.

Agent: I am sorry to hear that, Mr. Mitchell. Four years is a long time and we really value your loyalty. Before I process the cancellation, may I ask what you primarily use StreamVault for?

Customer: Mostly movies and the odd documentary. But at $18 a month it is just not worth it anymore.

Agent: I completely understand. I would love to keep you as a customer and I do have some options I can offer. Given your four years with us, I can offer you our Essential plan at $9 a month — that covers all movies and documentaries. Would that address your concern?

Customer: That is a significant saving. What would I lose?

Agent: You would lose access to live sports and premium series, but based on what you described, you do not use those. You would keep everything you actually watch.

Customer: Hmm. That is actually reasonable. And you can do that right now?

Agent: Yes, I can switch you over immediately. The new price takes effect on your next billing date. You would keep your current access until then with no interruption.

Customer: Alright, let us do that instead. I appreciate you not just processing the cancellation.

Agent: Of course. I have switched your plan to Essential at $9 a month effective your next billing cycle. I am also adding one free month as a thank you for your continued loyalty. Is there anything else I can help you with?

Customer: No, that is great. Thank you, Nina.

Agent: Thank you for staying with us, Mr. Mitchell. Enjoy your movies!
